assumptions.assumption: Monolithic backend with modular package structure (not microservices) for MVP
assumptions.rationale: Reduces operational complexity. Can extract to microservices later if needed.

assumptions.assumption: JWT-based authentication with refresh tokens, RBAC via database roles/permissions
assumptions.rationale: Standard enterprise pattern. SSO/OIDC can be added later via Spring Security OAuth2.

assumptions.assumption: Multi-company via organization_id FK on all domain entities (shared schema, row-level isolation)
assumptions.rationale: Matches current Odoo company_id pattern. Simpler than schema-per-tenant.

assumptions.assumption: PostgreSQL 16, UUID primary keys, soft delete, optimistic locking via @Version
assumptions.rationale: UUIDs avoid enumeration attacks and simplify distributed scenarios. Soft delete preserves audit trail.

assumptions.assumption: 1C integration via REST API (push/pull) with idempotency keys and reconciliation journal
assumptions.rationale: More reliable than direct DB sync. Standard for modern 1C integrations.

assumptions.assumption: EDO integration via Diadoc/SBIS REST APIs with document queue and retry mechanism
assumptions.rationale: Industry standard EDO operators in Russia.

assumptions.assumption: SPA with React, server-side rendering NOT required for internal ERP
assumptions.rationale: Internal tool, no SEO requirements. SPA provides best UX for data-heavy workflows.

assumptions.assumption: PDF reports generated server-side via JasperReports or Apache POI for Excel
assumptions.rationale: Russian regulatory forms (KS-2, KS-3) require precise formatting. JasperReports handles this well.

assumptions.assumption: Files stored in MinIO (S3-compatible) with metadata in PostgreSQL
assumptions.rationale: Scalable, can migrate to cloud S3 later. Supports versioning and large files.

assumptions.assumption: All user-facing text in Russian. Internationalization infrastructure present but not populated for other languages.
assumptions.rationale: Primary market is Russia. i18n can be added later if needed.

assumptions.assumption: Data migration from Odoo will be handled as a separate phase via ETL scripts reading Odoo PostgreSQL directly
assumptions.rationale: Keeps new system clean during development. Migration scripts developed after feature parity.

assumptions.assumption: Docker Compose for initial deployment, Kubernetes-ready but not required for MVP
assumptions.rationale: Docker Compose sufficient for single-server deployment. K8s adds complexity without immediate benefit.

assumptions.assumption: Project-level access control: users see only projects they are members of or all projects if ADMIN role
assumptions.rationale: Matches construction industry practice where teams are project-scoped.

assumptions.assumption: Full audit trail on all domain entities: who changed what, when, old/new values
assumptions.rationale: Regulatory requirement for construction companies. Implemented via JPA entity listeners.

assumptions.assumption: Module implementation order: Auth -> Org -> Projects -> Contracts -> Estimates -> Procurement -> KS-2/KS-3 -> Finance -> Documents -> Planning -> Analytics -> Integrations
assumptions.rationale: Each module depends on previous ones. Start with core domain, expand outward.

assumptions.assumption: Read-only DB audit skipped because psycopg is not installed.
assumptions.rationale: Database credentials or connectivity were unavailable during automated read-only audit.
